item.title: The Nightcliff by-election is this weekend. Here is why it's happening and how to vote
item.description: Residents living in Darwin's northern suburbs of Nightcliff, Rapid Creek and parts of Coconut Grove will be heading to the polls this Saturday to vote in a by-election. It comes after the former member for Nightcliff resigned in February.

item.title: Iran football chief pessimistic about FIFA World Cup following US air strikes
item.description: Reverberations from the Middle East conflict hits global sport, with Iran's soccer chief offering a bleak outlook for the FIFA World Cup in the United States.

item.title: Delta Goodrem to represent Australia at 2026 Eurovision
item.description: The Australian pop singer will perform her new single Eclipse at the 70th Eurovision Song Contest in Vienna in May to a global audience of more than 160 million viewers.

item.title: 'Bittersweet' victory for traditional owners after mining damage to East Kimberley burial site
item.description: A sacred site in the remote East Kimberley has been declared an Aboriginal heritage protected area, years after burial grounds were damaged by a local mining company.

item.title: Randa Abdel-Fattah appears at alternative Adelaide Writers' Week event
item.description: Palestinian Australian author Randa Abdel-Fattah has appeared at an alternative Adelaide Writers' Week event, telling a sold-out crowd journalists "muted" and "censored" Palestinian people amid the Gaza war.

item.title: NRL suspends Cowboys winger Braidon Burns after Las Vegas opener
item.description: North Queensland's Las Vegas hangover will extend well beyond a shock loss to Newcastle, with Braidon Burns receiving a two-match ban.

item.title: Crocodile spotted in suburban Newcastle creek captured
item.description: The sub-adult freshwater crocodile was first spotted on Saturday in a suburban creek. Reptile experts captured it on Sunday night and police are investigating how it came to be in Newcastle.

item.title: Lawyers for NRL player Zac Lomax and Parramatta Eels continue last-minute negotiations over breakup
item.description: Zac Lomax's NRL future remains in limbo as his lawyers conduct last-minute talks to secure his release from Parramatta.

item.title: Wilcannia Weir project delayed for decades gets delayed again by funding shortfall
item.description: An outback community on the Darling River has been calling for a new town weir for decades. After numerous delays, the Wilcannia Weir has hit another hurtle with the state government blaming the federal government for the funding shortfall.

item.title: Daniil Medvedev among tennis stars stranded in Dubai after air strikes, Zimbabwe cricketers stuck in India
item.description: Daniil Medvedev says he is unsure when he will be able to leave Dubai after the exchange of air strikes between the US, Israel and Iran shut down the UAE airport.

item.title: Actor Awards winners list: Sinners soars, Catherine O'Hara honoured and remembered
item.description: The Studio co-creator Seth Rogen accepted the award on O'Hara's behalf, following the death of the comedy legend in January.

item.title: Motorcyclist who died during SA downpour was on memorial ride for his dad
item.description: An outback tragedy has claimed the life of avid motorcyclist Darran Hyman, whose death during SA's weekend wet weather came almost exactly two years after his own father was also killed during a ride.

item.title: Former patients of gynaecologist Simon Gordon not included in Epworth Hospital review
item.description: Women who had surgery for "severe" endometriosis despite pathology showing little trace of the disease are angry and upset that Epworth Hospital has not invited them to participate in an external review.

item.title: Iran claims US and Israel attacked a girls' school. This is what is known about the strike
item.description: Iran says the death toll from an attack on a girls' school it claims was launched by the US and Israel has surpassed 165.

item.title: 'Cyclonic' dust storm blankets northern WA town of Onslow
item.description: A photographer from a coastal mining town in WA described the "cyclonic" winds as a thick dust storm blanketed her town.

item.title: NSW health minister rejects 'negligence' claims after snap review into hospitals
item.description: The review comes after a fungal infection cluster at a transplant ward at Sydney's Royal Prince Alfred Hospital linked to two deaths.

item.title: ARL Commission chair Peter V'landys says NRL's Las Vegas round will not lose its attraction
item.description: ARL Commission chair Peter V'Landys is adamant Las Vegas can remain a success beyond 2027 and insists fans will continue turning up once their team returns for a second time.

item.title: IBAC apologises to family violence victim for mishandling complaints about Victoria Police
item.description: IBAC's public apology to Jana Katerinskaja is a sharp reminder of the struggle for accountability victims of police-perpetrated family violence too often face.

item.title: Five of our best autumn recipes
item.description: We’ve crunched the numbers and these are five of your favourite ever autumn recipes to look forward to this season.

item.title: Economists warn Middle East war to hit households through oil price spike but say fallout could be short-lived
item.description: The average household could see $14 added to their weekly petrol bill, a prominent economist has warned. While oil prices have already spiked, analysts are hopeful the conflict will be short-lived, limiting the fallout.

item.title: Liberals give conditional support to free school plan, as Labor pledges OSHC expansion
item.description: Labor has announced a plan for further funding to expand out of school hours care, while the Liberal Party now supports the concept of free schools and is demanding a freight bypass to get trucks off city roads.

item.title: Tasmania's largest lamb exporter loses biggest market in the wake of Iran war
item.description: A meat exporter among those caught in the fallout of the US-Israeli bombing of Iran says his business will have to freeze hundreds of thousands of dollars' worth of produce.

item.title: Hanson censured over no 'good' Muslims comment, Coalition votes against
item.description: Two Liberal senators cross the floor to vote for Labor's motion that censured the One Nation leader for "inflammatory and divisive comments" that sought to vilify Muslim Australians.

item.title: NSW government grants six-year extension to controversial Dartbrook coal mine
item.description: The NSW government grants a six-year extension to a controversial coal mine in the Hunter Valley.

item.title: Coalition brands Australian children in Syria terrorist 'sympathisers'
item.description: Opposition Leader Angus Taylor has escalated his attack on the federal government over its response to the so-called "ISIS brides" cohort.

item.title: Weekend of war: Key moments from the first 48 hours of strikes in the Middle East
item.description: As the war in the Middle East escalates, ABC NEWS Verify has gathered verified video and satellite imagery in an early attempt to piece together some of the key events of the past 48 hours.

item.title: Backlash deepens over AI seatbelt cameras as drivers fined for passengers’ behaviour
item.description: Frustration is deepening over drivers being ordered to pay hundreds of dollars in fines for infringements incurred by their passengers — including young children and neurodivergent people not wearing seatbelts correctly.

item.title: Hawthorn co-captain Jai Newcombe gets one-week ban for rough conduct, Hawks to appeal
item.description: Gun midfielder Jai Newcombe will challenge his one-match ban for rough conduct in a bid to play in Hawthorn's season opener against GWS.

item.title: Iran beaten in opening game of Women's AFC Asian Cup as tensions escalate back home
item.description: Playing despite heightened tensions back home, Iran is thrashed 3-0 by 2022 runners-up South Korea in the team's opening match of the tournament.

item.title: Australia's gender pay gap narrows but gender-segregated industries persist, WGEA finds
item.description: Is your employer among the those with the largest or smallest gender pay gaps? More than 5.9 million workers from over 10,000 employers can now see the data for themselves.

item.title: Israel deploys troops into southern Lebanon marking an escalation against Hezbollah — as it happened
item.description: The United States and Israel are continuing to launch strikes at Iran, as Donald Trump tells Tehran's remaining power brokers it is "too late" to negotiate.

item.title: I knew I needed to call off my wedding, I just didn't know why
item.description: For Sashi, calling off a destination wedding with 250 guests was hard. But it was the start of a long journey towards learning about her mental health.

item.title: Strikes on Iran throw spanner in the works for inflation as oil price surges
item.description: Before trading got underway on Monday, there was something of a bidding war on how far oil prices could surge, beginning at $US100 a barrel, right through to $US150. But there's plenty of time for things to turn pear-shaped.

item.title: Switzerland can save its alpine villages. But should it?
item.description: The Swiss Alps are a natural wonder and a key part of the national identity, but life in the mountains is under threat from climate change.

item.title: Cut red tape, tax to fix Australia's middling investment rates, business lobbies say
item.description: A new index compiled by the Business Council of Australia shows Australia in the middle of the pack as an investment destination.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Uzbekistan investing in women's football as it prepares to host in 2029
item.description: Uzbekistan returns to the Women's Asian Cup for the first time in 23 years, hoping this is the start of a new era for women's football as it prepares to host the next tournament in 2029.

item.title: Victoria leads Australia in media suppression order numbers, raising public trust concerns
item.description: Victorian courts lead the nation in the number of suppression orders handed down, with more than one order handed down every day in the state.

item.title: Parties raise federal campaign funds amid South Australia's political donation ban
item.description: Political experts say the use of South Australian political party websites to fundraise for federal campaign purposes raises questions about the risk and perceptions of influence amid new state bans.

item.title: From rule changes to new wildcard round, here's what's new in AFL for 2026
item.description: The new AFL season is only days away, and there are plenty of new things to get your head around. Get up to speed before Thursday night's opener.

item.title: Yorta Yorta leaders claim they were not consulted on $13m project that disturbed ancestral remains
item.description: Traditional owners in northern Victoria say they were not consulted about a project where human remains, believed to be an ancestral burial, have been unearthed.

item.title: Trump lists four goals in Iran as Middle East war widens
item.description: Speaking at his first White House appearance since the joint US-Israeli attack began, the US president says he seized "our last, best chance" to strike Iran's "sick and sinister regime".

item.title: Booroolong frogs return to north-west NSW waterways thanks to breeding program
item.description: Hidden in the dark of night, more than 1,000 endangered frogs have been released back into waterways in north-west NSW.

item.title: A year on from floods, north Queensland retirees yet to return home due to delays to insurance repairs
item.description: It was meant to be their retirement dream, but north Queenslanders have been out of their homes for over a year awaiting flood insurance repairs.

item.title: Millions promised for mental health housing support undelivered, years after royal commission
item.description: The Victorian government is accused of abandoning a funding commitment intended to stop Victorians living with mental illness from falling through the cracks of the housing crisis.

item.title: Good looks drive $235,000 record-breaking sale of South Australian Angus bull
item.description: Perfect skin, good looks and a sparkling personality may sound like a romantic partner wish list, but this is what buyers are after when selecting a "complete" bull.

item.title: Treasurer asks ACCC to monitor fuel prices amid Middle East conflict — as it happened
item.description: Treasurer Jim Chalmers has written to the Australian Competition and Consumer Commission asking it to monitor fuel prices amid the unrest in the Middle East. Take a look back on the day's events.

item.title: Queensland Reds enforcer Lukhan Salakaia-Loto shines to start Super Rugby Pacific season
item.description: Playing with a chip on his shoulder and a point to prove to national selectors, Lukhan Salakaia-Loto is not wasting any opportunities early in the Super Rugby Pacific season.

item.title: Nightcliff by-election will be a test for Territory Labor. Can it win it back?
item.description: Territory Labor lost the previously safe seat of Nightcliff as it was bundled out of government. Eighteen months on, does it deserve to win the seat back?

item.title: RBA governor says 'too early to say' how Middle East war will impact Australia's economy
item.description: Michelle Bullock says events are moving rapidly and there are different ways the war could play out, with RBA staff closely monitoring the situation.

item.title: National Farmers' Federation vice-president refuses to step down over 'unacceptable' social media posts
item.description: The National Farmers' Federation board has asked its vice-president to reconsider his position after he made "unacceptable" social media posts.

item.title: Rebuilding the scorched nesting hollows of endangered Carnaby's black cockatoos
item.description: Conservationists are trying to put in place a plan to rehabilitate a key nesting area for endangered black cockatoos that was ravaged by fire.

item.title: Artists' work at risk as researcher urges privacy checks over AI caricature trend
item.description: Amateur artist Anne Rowlands lost all her commissions in the past year and says the rise of AI-generated "art" is to blame.

item.title: Redness on Trump's neck caused by common skin cream, White House says
item.description: The White House says the large red spot on US President Donald Trump's neck is due to a skin cream he is using, without elaborating on what condition it is treating.

item.title: ADF personnel are 'safe' after air base strike near Dubai, Defence Minister Richard Marles says
item.description: Defence Minister Richard Marles says Australian personnel are safe and accounted for after an air base used by Australian forces was hit by Iranian drones near Dubai.

item.title: Eels and Storm reach shock out-of-court settlement over Zac Lomax NRL saga
item.description: Parramatta and Melbourne reach an out-of-court settlement that leaves Zac Lomax unable to join the Storm until 2028.

item.title: Calls for review into Queensland fire truck purchases after hundreds of complaints made in 10 months
item.description: Volunteers say they are being sent to fire grounds in vehicles that fail to meet basic safety requirements.

item.title: Satellite images show destruction at huge oil refinery, Iranian nuclear facility
item.description: Newly released satellite imagery shows the aftermath of an Iranian drone attack targeting one of the world's largest oil refineries.

item.title: Growers branch out as demand grows for unique Australian native flowers
item.description: Sturt's desert pea may be native to dry, arid conditions but that hasn't stopped growers on the NSW coast from giving it a crack, as demand grows for unique Australian flowers.

item.title: Chris Scott welcomes Tanner Bruhn's return to Cats after 'egregious injustice' of rape charge
item.description: Geelong coach Chris Scott confirms Tanner Bruhn will play his first AFL game in 17 months after being sidelined due to rape and intentional sexual touching charges.

item.title: Fisheries authorities investigate video of grey nurse shark caught by fishers at Terrigal Haven
item.description: At least one grey nurse shark is dead and others have been injured by fishers on the NSW Central Coast.

item.title: Australian agriculture hits $100b target four years early but production set to dip next year
item.description: The value of Australia's farming produce is tipped to reach its "ambitious" $100 billion target this financial year, but experts warn the value could dip again in the face of trying trade and seasonal conditions.

item.title: Israel's ambassador to Australia seeks to discredit the United Nations
item.description: Israel's new ambassador to Australia seeks to discredit the United Nations by claiming the country "can't take them seriously".

item.title: Smoke billows from oil refinery in Ras Tanura
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tina Arena, Ngaiire reimagine Sia's Unstoppable, open up about life-saving blood donors
item.description: Music artists Tina Arena and Ngaiire have reimagined Sia's Unstoppable while opening up about how life-saving donated blood brought them back from the brink of serious illness.

item.title: Matildas star Amy Sayer lauds bravery of Iran players ahead of Asian Cup clash
item.description: The Matildas are expressing their sympathy while sharing a hotel with Iran's players ahead of this week's crucial Asian Cup clash.

item.title: Can you ask a neighbour to remove or trim a large tree for safety or other concerns?
item.description: Whether you're worried about how old mate's tree next door will hold up in the summer storms, or it's just creating more work for you on the weekend, knowing where you stand can be helpful.

item.title: Economist blames ACT's fiscal deterioration 'entirely' on policy decisions as Barr defends spending
item.description: An independent economist has laid the blame for the ACT's financial woes at the feet of the government, saying it's "entirely" due to policy decisions. But even he agrees the territory is uniquely challenged in its ability to raise revenue.

item.title: Hidden review slams senior Liberals' lack of reflection after devastating election loss
item.description: Senior decision makers responsible for the Liberal Party's disastrous federal election defeat largely failed to critically assess their own performance in the aftermath, according to a controversial internal review.

item.title: Australian triathlete stranded in Saudi Arabia amid conflict in Middle East
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Aussie travellers trapped in Middle East recount missile strikes and late-night evacuations
item.description: Aussie travellers trapped in the Middle East recount missile strikes and late-night evacuations as thousands remain stranded with no end in sight.

item.title: Teenager pleads guilty to threatening to kill prime minister, NSW premier on social media
item.description: A 19-year-old WA man admits threatening to kill the PM and NSW premier in a direct message to Chris Minns on Instagram.

item.title: Threats to crucial Strait of Hormuz pack pressure on global oil, gas and freight prices
item.description: Iran's threats to attack vessels in one of the most important shipping passages will strain global supply of oil and gas, experts say — and Australia will not be immune from the fallout.

item.title: Vision of explosion in Beirut
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: After years in the wilderness, North Korea lays down a marker in Western Sydney
item.description: Absent from the tournament for 16 years, women's football powerhouse North Korea opens its Asian Cup campaign with victory over Uzbekistan.

item.title: Justis Huni set for return on Tyson Fury fight card in first boxing match since trainer's death
item.description: Justis Huni will make his long-awaited return to the boxing ring in London as a part of Tyson Fury's fight card.

item.title: Jackie O announces departure from breakfast show she hosted with Kyle Sandilands
item.description: In an ASX announcement, ARN Media saysHenderson "has given notice she cannot continue to work with Mr Sandilands".

item.title: Vision of Iranian missile strike in Be'er Sheva
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Travis Head rejects claims Australia didn't care about T20 World Cup after early exit
item.description: Travis Head hits back at the public narrative that has followed Australia's surprise early exit from the T20 World Cup.

item.title: Family doctor had 'sexual motivation' for performing pap smears, tribunal finds
item.description: A Queensland former doctor has been indefinitely disqualified from reapplying for registration after a tribunal found he excessively performed pap smears, breast checks and vaginal examinations on female patients.

item.title: Domestic violence prevention sector rejects NT government's mandatory sentencing bill
item.description: The Northern Territory government's plan to increase the mandatory minimum sentence for domestic violence murder has been panned by the prevention sector, as the NT attorney-general appears to backtrack on earlier remarks.

item.title: Tuesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: Sunken illegal fishing vessel near Masig
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Who will be Iran’s next Supreme Leader?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Switzerland's Crumbling Giants: The alpine towns under threat
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Damage in Beirut's southern suburbs after Israeli strikes
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Donald Trump attempts to ease global financial market panic via Truth Social post
item.description: Donald Trump has posted on his Truth Social account the US Navy will escort ships through the Strait of Hormuz. It has arrested some of the panic on global financial markets.

item.title: Listeners to Kyle and Jackie O noticed shift in relationship long before it imploded
item.description: Jackie "O" Henderson has given notice saying she can no longer work with Kyle Sandilands, bringing an end to the Kyle and Jackie O Show.

item.title: Blood moon total lunar eclipse lights up Australian sky
item.description: Australians looking to the sky on Tuesday night saw the Moon turn a reddish-orange hue, in the last total lunar eclipse until 2029.

item.title: First repatriation flight lands in Sydney, nearly 150 missing as Iranian ship sinks — as it happened
item.description: Several people dead and nearly 150 missing after Iranian warship sinks off Sri Lanka. The sinking comes as the first repatriation flight from Dubai lands in Sydney.

item.title: Racing minister’s office aware of Tasracing's TasInsure message that may have breached caretaker conventions
item.description: In the middle of another Tasmanian election campaign, the state's racing participants received a text message from a government body promoting a Liberal policy — an apparent breach of caretaker convention.

item.title: How the US and Israel struck Iran in Operation Epic Fury
item.description: Launched in broad daylight after weeks of intelligence gathering, Israel and the US's attack on Iran was designed to ensure "speed, surprise, and violence of action". Here's how it unfolded.

item.title: Billionaire James Packer's $150,000 donation to Advance a 'worrying' sign for Liberals
item.description: Liberals say billionaire James Packer's first political donation in 12 years to a conservative lobby group should be a cause for concern, saying the rise of Advance could push the party further from the mainstream.

item.title: NDIS ordered to fund mobility scooter in 'extremely significant' Federal Court ruling
item.description: The agency running the NDIS has lost a legal case experts say could have significant implications for how future funding decisions are made.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Japan's quiet transformation has left it poised to challenge for the trophy
item.description: With a host of international players and a recent tournament win, Japan has quietly redefined its identity in the hope of lifting the 2026 AFC Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: Improved facilities coming to Tasmania's Maria Island, but some fear loss of charm as well
item.description: Major upgrades are underway at Maria Island, but many visitors don't want the popular Tasmanian destination to change to accommodate rising tourist numbers.

item.title: Earth at night from the International Space Station.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Thousands of satellites are orbiting Earth.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Best rain in six months a relief for drought-hit southern NSW farmers
item.description: Heavy downpours across southern and central New South Wales have felt "like raining headache relief pills", according to farmers who were beginning to destock due to drought.

item.title: Donald Trump's messages on Iran are mixed, and it means Operation Epic Fury could be an epic failure
item.description: What is the White House's justification for authorising US military strikes on Iran? Right now, the answer changes every day.

item.title: Australian aged care facilities unprepared for bushfire evacuations, union warns
item.description: The vast majority of aged care facilities would be unable to evacuate in the case of a bushfire, according to the Australian Workers Union.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Iran's footballers playing on in Australia, amid war in Middle East
item.description: As war spreads across the Middle East, Iran's women footballers are playing on at the Women's Asian Cup, having already faced a challenging road to make it to Australia.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Vietnam typifies perseverance as it aims for new heights
item.description: Vietnam's women's football team has spent the past 20 years patiently building success out of the spotlight, and is ready to take centre stage at the 2026 Women's Asian Cup.

item.title: A fungal disease on an iconic Brisbane lizard species has researchers worried
item.description: A fungal disease affecting Brisbane's water dragons could be a big deal for Australian reptiles, researchers say

item.title: ACT Shelter 'surviving on reserves' after serving Canberra's homeless community for 50 years
item.description: A not-for-profit housing organisation that has been helping low-income and homeless Canberrans for over 50 years says its future is at risk after having its funding almost halved.

item.title: First track map issued for possible North Queensland cyclone
item.description: There is a moderate chance a tropical low in the Coral Sea will develop into a cyclone by the end of this week.

item.title: KIIS FM radio's Kyle and Jackie O split after decades of controversies
item.description: Kyle and Jackie O have been some of Australia's most controversial broadcasters over the past two decades. Here are their top three controversial moments on air.

item.title: Children across SA jump for joy as they soak up best rain in years
item.description: Most of us have memories of jumping in big rain puddles, or making mud pies growing up, but for many regional and rural kids in SA last weekend's rain event was the first time they had popped their gumboots on.

item.title: Global stock sell-off hits ASX, trade halted in Korea as market plunges — as it happened
item.description: Investor fears of a drawn-out Middle East war, surging oil prices and potentially higher inflation are hitting Australian shares, with the ASX down 2 per cent. Panic selling hits Korea's market, triggering a circuit breaker.

item.title: Federal government's plan for Darwin Port criticised by former NT treasurer Dave Tollner
item.description: Former NT treasurer Dave Tollner describes the federal government's plans to take back the 99-year lease of Darwin Port as "an appalling waste of money".

item.title: Donald Trump says 'just about everything's been knocked out' in Iran
item.description: The US president says Iran's navy, air force and air detection systems have been "knocked out", as Israeli and US forces keep pounding targets across the country.

item.title: Jeremy Rockliff's State of the State address flags public service job cuts but spruiks 'exciting days ahead'
item.description: For a debt-ridden state facing cuts to public service jobs, you'd hardly know it by listening to the rosy pictures being painted by Tasmania's premier.

item.title: Players flee court at UAE tennis event after security alert amid Middle East war
item.description: An ATP Challenger tournament in Fujairah is halted abruptly due to a security alert, forcing players and officials to run from the courts to designated safe areas.

item.title: Case of WA terror accused Jayson Joseph Michaels highlights limitations of WA gun laws
item.description: Western Australia is transitioning to tougher firearms laws — but despite the changes, 20-year-old Jayson Joseph Michaels and his father were allowed to keep their collection of seven guns at the family's Bindoon home.

item.title: Why Melbourne Storm have been waiting a generation for a player like Sua Fa'alogo
item.description: Sua Fa'alogo has been waiting for some time to become part of Melbourne's storied lineage of fullbacks, but nowhere near as long as Melbourne has been waiting for a player like him.

item.title: Australian racing driver Joanne Ciconte to compete in Japan, chasing F1 dream
item.description: Driven to reach the highest heights in motorsport, Australian teenager Joanne Ciconte sets her sights on racing in Japan.

item.title: Barunga Festival 2026 under new management as previous agency claims being 'blindsided'
item.description: The major annual festival of Indigenous art, culture, sport and music will be run by new organisers this year, with previous event planner Sprout saying it was "blindsided" by the change in direction.

item.title: Artificial intelligence detects high breast cancer risk in women given all-clear
item.description: A new Australian artificial intelligence tool could transform breast cancer screening by offering a more personalised and accurate way to identify risk.

item.title: Ex-Wallabies coach Dave Rennie to lead All Blacks through to 2027 Rugby World Cup
item.description: Three years after he was sacked as Wallabies coach, Dave Rennie is put in charge of the All Blacks for the next two seasons.

item.title: Marine tracking data shows oil tankers 'down to zero' in Strait of Hormuz
item.description: The Strait of Hormuz normally has 50 ships moving over 100 metres, but today there are virtually zero.

item.title: Australia's economy growing at fastest rate in almost three years
item.description: Australia's economy is growing at annual rate of 2.6 per cent, which is much stronger than forecast.

item.title: US Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick agrees to interview on Epstein ties
item.description: Trump appointee Howard Lutnick agrees to a transcribed interview with the House Oversight Committee over his past ties to convicted child sex offender Jeffrey Epstein.

item.title: Adelaide Festival 2026, featuring Pulp and The Cherry Orchard, explores censorship and class
item.description: With the potential to be overshadowed by the cancellation of its writers' festival, Adelaide Festival opened over the weekend, bringing together local and international artists.

item.title: Walgett Shire demands urgent action over asbestos stockpiles near homes
item.description: The remote western NSW communities have been waiting on the removal of 40,000 cubic metres of asbestos material for years.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O's reported $200m pay deal under a cloud after end of long-running show
item.description: The demise of Kyle Sandilands and Jackie "O" Henderson's radio show likely marks the end of big-name stars on long-running multi-million-dollar contracts dominating Australian airwaves, media commentators say.

item.title: Big smiles for farmers as downpour soaks parched paddocks in Victoria
item.description: A big downpour across much of drought-affected Victoria puts smiles on farmers' faces.

item.title: Trump says UK's Starmer is no Winston Churchill after rift over Iran strikes
item.description: US President Donald Trump has criticised British Prime Minister Sir Keir Starmer over his reluctance to join the US-Israeli strikes on Iran, saying, "This is not Winston Churchill that we're dealing with."

item.title: From hiring limousines to $10k flights, Australians go to extremes to escape Middle East war
item.description: Australian travellers stuck overseas take extraordinary measures to get home amid the escalating war in the Middle East.

item.title: Dennis Cometti, legendary sports commentator, dies aged 76
item.description: Dennis Cometti — who called a range of sports for the ABC, Seven and Nine and was lauded for his football commentary over 50-plus years in the industry — is remembered as "the best of his kind" after his death aged 76.

item.title: SA Police confirm two members of Gus Lamont's family 'not cooperating' directly with police
item.description: South Australia's Police Commissioner has said that two members of missing boy Gus Lamont's family are "not cooperating" with investigators, but a police spokesperson said they could not comment further on the number of suspects in the case.

item.title: Albanese government did not act on advice to halt salmon farming at Macquarie Harbour
item.description: Former environment minister Tanya Plibersek was advised by her department to revoke salmon farming approvals in Tasmania's Macquarie Harbour amid concerns about the industry's impact on the endangered Maugean skate, FOI documents show.

item.title: Adelaide Fringe ticket sales 'drop off a cliff' because of fizzer rain forecast
item.description: A dire weather forecast for last weekend in Adelaide combine with ongoing cost of living pressures to deliver a huge drop-off in Adelaide Fringe ticket sales, organisers and artists say.

item.title: Cuba charges six exiles with terrorism over speedboat attack
item.description: Cuban prosecutors charge six people with "crimes of terrorism" after a speedboat in Cuban waters allegedly opened fire on a border guard vessel last week.

item.title: Netball teams ousted from league due to lack of footballers sparks equal opportunity complaint
item.description: Victoria's Equal Opportunity and Human Rights Commission has been contacted after a number of junior netball teams were axed from the Ballarat Football Netball league due to a lack of footballers.

item.title: Body found during search for tourist missing from Gold Coast beach
item.description: Police say emergency services were called after reports a 20-year-old man, who is believed to be a tourist, was seen struggling in the water off Main Beach.

item.title: Children reported to Queensland Health over 'unlicensed' cupcake stand
item.description: Kelly Stothar says she was flabbergasted when she received a letter from Brisbane City Council threatening her with fines over her children's cupcake stand.

item.title: Man arrested in missing persons case of Brendan Breen, football hero's brother
item.description: Police have made an arrest in relation to the long-running missing persons case of the fraudster brother of a former St Kilda footballer.

item.title: Legendary sports broadcaster Dennis Cometti has died aged 76, his family has confirmed.
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: By striking Gulf states Iran is hitting one of Donald Trump's vulnerabilities
item.description: Despite their immense wealth — and even their past capacity to influence the globe through their control over oil prices — the Gulf states don't have much ability to influence events in this war.

item.title: Cloud Carrier proposes gas-fired power station to power Moss Vale AI data centre
item.description: A small town in the NSW Southern Highlands is watching the AI revolution take shape in its backyard, as plans emerge to build one of the state's largest gas-fired power stations to run a data-processing complex.

item.title: Footage of several strikes in Tehran's Farmanieh district
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Australian crisis teams sent to Middle East as Iran war continues
item.description: The federal government is sending six crisis response teams to the Middle East to help thousands of Australians stranded in the region, while the foreign minister continues to brush off questions about whether the US and Israel's strikes on Iran were legal.

item.title: Middle East conflict hits meat exports, pushes up costs for farmers
item.description: Exports have been halted and shipping containers filled with Australian meat are stranded at sea as the Middle East conflict takes a toll on trade.

item.title: ARN Media's $200m Kyle and Jackie O experiment has failed
item.description: The network's mammoth deal banked on the fact that people in Melbourne would take to the hosts just as Sydneysiders did.

item.title: 'Centimetre perfect': Revisiting Dennis Cometti's best one-liners as AFL community mourns commentary icon
item.description: As the AFL community mourns the death of one of its most iconic voices, here are some of the most memorable Dennis Cometti one-liners.

item.title: Tim Picton's accused killer Brodie Jake Dewar released on bail
item.description: Brodie Jake Dewar, 20, who allegedly killed Labor identity Tim Picton after striking him and causing him to fall over, is granted bail by a Perth judge.

item.title: Your photos of the blood moon from around Australia
item.description: Stargazers across the country have shared photos of the blood moon total lunar eclipse, from the city to the beach and regional backyards — here's a look.

item.title: Homes Tasmania, social housing body, axed after failing to deliver on homes target
item.description: Tasmania's state-owned public housing authority, criticised as being "hamstrung by confused decision making", gets the chop after failing to deliver on its goals in its three years of existence.

item.title: Driver captures unfolding police incident
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Tim Wilson's inflation fire ditty ahead of Question Time
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Disgraced dentist Steven Lin identified as man shot dead by police in Sydney's Potts Point
item.description: Steven Lin allegedly attacked two women while armed with a knife inside a Sydney apartment complex before being shot dead by police.

item.title: Petrol prices surge across Australia, long queues at fuel stations amid Middle East war
item.description: Petrol retailers across the nation are being accused of "unconscionable" behaviour by preying on people's anxieties as fuel prices surge in the midst of the Middle East war.

item.title: Cost of new Melbourne F1 Grand Prix pit building soars due to asbestos work
item.description: The planned rebuild of a pit lane at Albert Park has blown out by millions of dollars, with Victorian taxpayers to foot the bill.

item.title: Fuel price nears $4 per litre in Arnhem Land, with further increases expected due to war in Middle East
item.description: Remote communities are paying nearly $4 per litre for diesel and NT residents are claiming the price has jumped unfairly this week.

item.title: How airlines determine if it is safe to fly from conflict zones
item.description: A flight from Dubai to Australia has taken off, becoming the first commercial plane to carry Australians home since the Middle East conflict began.

item.title: Why do some of us vividly remember dreams and others say they 'don't dream'?
item.description: Some people are naturally better at recalling their dreams. Others worry they're not getting quality sleep if they can't remember their dreams. Here's the science.

item.title: Dennis Cometti speaks to the ABC in 1984
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Dennis Cometti's commentary made him a legend, but dedication off the mic defined him
item.description: Dennis Cometti was known to viewers as a wordsmith who rattled off rejoinders with ease, but behind the scenes he worked harder than anyone to carve out a place in Australia's sporting landscape.

item.title: Housing ACT failing tenants by leaving properties in disrepair, ombudsman says
item.description: The ACT Ombudsman says the ACT government is failing its legal obligations as a landlord after looking into complaints by tenants.

item.title: Iran brings exploding drone boats into war in attack on oil tanker
item.description: Iran is using exploding drone boats for the first time in war in the Middle East, hitting an oil tanker near the Strait of Hormuz after ordering the key shipping passage closed.

item.title: Mosman Park murder family friend speaks of ‘harrowing’ reality raising child with severe autism
item.description: Simon Lewis knew the Clune family, who died in an apparent murder-suicide last month, and says his own experience with his severely autistic daughter means he can relate "to what they were going through".

item.title: Gold Coast business suspected of Victorian underworld ties, CFMEU inquiry commissioner says
item.description: A Queensland business is suspected of ties to Victorian underworld figure Mick Gatto by Queensland's CFMEU Inquiry.

item.title: Bruce McAvaney mourns Dennis Cometti, hailing fellow AFL commentator his 'ultimate teammate'
item.description: Bruce McAvaney says he has "lost something truly precious" with the death of long-time AFL commentary partner Dennis Cometti.

item.title: Islamic community group Taha Humanity Association's grant cancelled amid 'social cohesion concerns'
item.description: The federal government has cancelled a grant for an Islamic community association in Melbourne citing "social cohesion concerns" after claims surfaced the group was mourning the death of Iran's supreme leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei.

item.title: Women's Asian Cup 2026: Iranian player fights back tears as she speaks about situation in Iran
item.description: On the eve of their Women's Asian Cup match against the Matildas, Iran's women's football team has expressed concern over the plight of their family and friends in Iran as war rages in the Middle East.

item.title: Wednesday finance with Alan Kohler
item.description: News from the world of finance and business. Top stories in text, video and audio. Business video news bulletin. Features and analysis.

item.title: What is next for the Middle East?
item.description: Sarah Ferguson presents Australia's premier daily current affairs program, delivering agenda-setting public affairs journalism and interviews that hold the powerful to account. Plus political analysis from Laura Tingle.

item.title: Kyle Sandilands and Jackie 'O' Henderson's former content director Craig Bruce tells of contract 'insanity'
item.description: Craig Bruce helped elevate Kyle Sandilands and Jackie O. He's watched on as the pair have fallen apart underneath the weight of the richest deal in Australian media.

item.title: Inquiry launched into anti-Indigenous racism after alleged Perth Invasion Day rally terror attempt
item.description: The attempted alleged bombing of an Invasion Day rally in Perth has sparked a parliamentary inquiry into racism, hate and violence directed at Indigenous people.

item.title: Budget blowouts, patient safety and 'toxic' workplace claims raised in inquiry into NT's Acacia patient record system
item.description: A hearing into the Northern Territory's controversial health patient record system Acacia has heard allegations of risks to patient safety and a "toxic" workplace culture.

item.title: WA shires say renewable community benefit fund needs to be mandatory
item.description: Shires in WA have welcomed the release of guidelines that could see them make significant money for hosting renewable energy projects, but there are questions about how to make sure the cash comes through.

item.title: Mark Carney warns Australia and Canada face 'subordination' by hegemonic powers
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney calls for middle powers to use their sovereignty in a Sydney speech echoing his Davos intervention.

item.title: Smoke and destruction in Azadi Square, Tehran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Emotional scenes as first commercial flight out of Middle East since war began lands safely in Sydney
item.description: Emirates flight EK414 from Dubai touched down safely with around 200 Australians on board, after departing Dubai in the early hours of the morning.

item.title: Live: More than 80 Iranian sailors dead after US strike on warship in Indian Ocean
item.description: The first flight from Dubai to Sydney has landed amid the ongoing conflict within the Middle East. Follow live.

item.title: Albanese supported the 'rules-based order' six months ago
item.description: Bipartisan support for the "global rules-based order" has all but disappeared in Australia.

item.title: Kyle and Jackie O shock tactics weren't a match for commercial and community pressures
item.description: The fact that we are now staring at the smoking embers of arguably Australia's most successful radio pairing surely must be linked to a pincer movement of increasingly uncomfortable commercial and community pressures.

item.title: Art offers us strength when the world feels unsteady
item.description: At a time when the world feels precarious, art can offer guidance on survival and resilience.

item.title: Tasmanian nurses say lower wages, understaffing a barrier to returning home
item.description: Tasmania needs more nurses, but lower wages and less opportunities for career growth remain barriers for enticing health workers to return to their home state.

item.title: Seasonal produce to make the most of in March
item.description: From apples to avocados, these are the early autumn fruit and veggies to look for.

item.title: ABC Places quiz: Where in the world is this?
item.description: Today's ABC Places quiz features photos from 10 locations around the world. Your challenge is to guess where you are.

item.title: Car buyers left tens of thousands out of pocket as platform Carconnect enters administration
item.description: Online car-buying platform Carconnect has entered voluntary administration, leaving questions about how its customers will collect their vehicles, and whether they will get their money back.

item.title: Google's AI response to fake or AI generated imagery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Google's AI response to fake or AI generated imagery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: ODYs_VerifyAIgen_0403
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Google's AI response to fake or AI generated imagery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Video game footage shared as vision from the latest conflict in Iran
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Google's AI response to fake or AI generated imagery
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Real attack or video game? Misinformation and war in the AI age
item.description: Is it real or is it AI? Misinformation seen by millions is spreading online in the wake of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Canada 'unlikely' to join AUKUS despite previous enthusiasm for pact
item.description: Canadian Prime Minister Mark Carney and senior Canadian administration figures are in Canberra with defence and security expected to dominate the discussions there.

item.title: Conservative party New Zealand First pushes to make English an official language
item.description: New Zealand might be full of English speakers, but some politicians are pushing for laws to protect the language.

item.title: SA SES uses drone to warn and check in on drivers during flood events
item.description: SA SES says it trialled drone technology for the first time when record rainfall lashed parts of the state over the weekend.

item.title: US military releases vision of torpedo strike on Iranian warship
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: What you need to know about Iran today, with Laura Tingle
item.description: The ABC's global affairs editor, Laura Tingle, is in Dubai and is providing daily updates on the Middle East war.

item.title: Australian Federal Police see growth in reports of forced marriage, survivor calls for change
item.description: A survivor of underage forced marriage, Helena Hassani is using her lived experience to raise critical awareness, amid a growth in reports of the crime in Australia.

item.title: Ingham and north Queensland bracing for rain, flash flooding threat as tropical low looms off coast
item.description: If a tropical low off Queensland is to become a cyclone, the weather bureau says today will be its best chance.

item.title: Australian regulator delays ban on plumbing parts containing lead by two years
item.description: The Australian Building Codes Board delays a ban on plumbing products containing lead by two more years, despite backlash from the industry.

item.title: SA Liberal leader Ashton Hurn facing similar wipe-out to WA counterpart Zak Kirkup in 2021
item.description: The Liberal wipe-out in WA five years ago is undoubtedly making uncomfortable reading for SA Liberal leader Ashton Hurn as she heads to the polls desperately hoping for at least one key difference.

item.title: The iconic 1980s Slip Slop Slap campaign
item.description: Follow the latest headlines from ABC News, Australia's most trusted media source, with live events, audio and on-demand video from the national broadcaster.

item.title: Hopes for lower melanoma risk as study finds number of moles on children's bodies halved in 25 years
item.description: The study — which surveyed almost 4,000 south-east Queensland children — found average mole count plummeted from 87 in 1992 to 46 in 2016 — a 47 per cent reduction.

item.title: Tasmanian salmon farming emergency licence for antibiotic florfenicol revoked over 'unacceptable risk'
item.description: The use of florfenicol in Tasmania's salmon industry has been banned as the country's veterinary medicine regulator suspends an emergency licence for the antibiotic.
